title: Brother, Can You Spare a Lead?
title_candidates: Brother, Can You Spare a Lead?
theme: Jon Baker built a cushion, then handed the Trophy to brother Alex over a brutal three-hole stretch on the back nine, while David Mullin's day-long misadventure dragged him to the bottom of the Spoon pile.
opening_hook: Open at the 1st green, where Alex Baker's opening bogey was somehow good enough to draw level with his brother — a quiet hint that the Trophy lead was already loose in its moorings before anyone had warmed up.
foreshadow: Alex drawing level at the 1st (r3_b09) is the soft setup — the lead is already share-able before Catalonia has finished its coffee.
Jon Baker's 10-point burst through 7-9 (r3_b07) looks like a coronation in real time, which makes the 11-13 collapse all the crueller.
David Mullin's quadruple at the 6th (r3_b04) is the first crack in a round that will eventually deposit him at the bottom of the Spoon table by the 12th.

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Lead changed hands. Jon Baker arrived in Barcelona two clear; Alex drew level at the 1st (r3_b09), Jon then surged ahead with a 10-point flurry at 7-9, but bled seven shots at 11-13 (r3_b12) and Alex took outright command at the 13th (r3_b03). Same 2-shot gap, opposite direction.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Lead held — but not without a wobble. Mullin briefly drew level at the 3rd (r3_b10) before his own card unravelled; Jon's bogey at the 6th (r3_b01) reconfirmed his outright lead, and the 3-shot margin over Mullin is unchanged from the start of the day.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: New occupant. Patterson held the Spoon overnight; Mullin's quadruple at the 6th and bogey at the 12th (r3_b06) completed the swap, and he now trails the field by the same 5 that Patterson did 24 hours earlier.

players.arc: Drew level at the 1st, sat patiently as his brother galloped clear, then capitalised on the 11-13 wobble with three straight pars of his own (r3_b08) to take the Trophy lead outright at the 13th.
Authored both halves of the day's main story: 10 points in three holes through 7-9, then seven shots leaked at 11-13 — kept the Jacket lead, lost the Trophy lead, all in the same round.
Quadrupled the 6th, briefly drew level for the Jacket at the 3rd, and by the 12th had slid past Patterson to occupy the Spoon position on his own.
A quiet round in the standings sense, save for an 8 at the 5th (r3_b05) that quietly killed any momentum he might have taken into the back nine.
Climbed off the bottom of the Spoon table almost by accident — a nine-point burst at 8-10 (r3_b11) doing the lifting, before a 9 at the 16th (r3_b02) reminded everyone he hadn't gone far.

venue_notes: El Prat's Azul routing — a Greg Norman parkland on TEG's second-ever visit to Catalonia and the course's debut on the schedule. Worth a line that nobody had local knowledge to lean on; the quad bogeys at the 5th and 6th suggest the opening stretch had teeth.
